since_last: Since 1:35 PM: SpaceX closed its first session at $163.11 (+20.8%) — thirty cents above the $162.81 open, the watch level held; semis faded into the bell; MU reversed red; NVDA flat again.

cards.headline: SPCX first close: $163.11, the debut bid held by 30 cents
cards.body: The biggest IPO in history closed day one +20.8% above the $135 pricing, at $163.11 — just above its $162.81 opening print after tagging $171.85 intraday and fading with the tape. Hyperliquid's perp tracks at ~$163.5 after hours, glued to spot all day. Cboe options arrive Tuesday — the first real two-way market for the name.
cards.watch: Monday holding above the $162.81 open print keeps the debut structure intact ahead of options listing.

cards.headline: Semis: big day, soft finish — and MU reversed
cards.body: SOXX closed +1.59% and SMH +1.72%, well off the +2.5% midday highs — strength sold into the bell. ARM +11.9% was the day's winner, INTC +6.8%, AMD +4.8%, QCOM +4.5% — but MU reversed an early gain to close −1.35%, and NVDA finished +0.09%, flat for a second straight session while its sector ran. Breadth 47/84 green.
cards.watch: Whether the into-the-close fade was profit-taking or the leg cooling — NVDA joining on Monday would answer it.

cards.headline: ADBE −6.7% at the bell: the software bleed is confirmed
cards.body: Adobe closed down −6.7% on day two (CFO departure + FY26 ARR uncertainty), with Apps & Data again the weakest sector while silicon led. Two full sessions of out-of-software, into-silicon rotation is now an established tape, not noise.

cards.headline: Into next week: two unresolved questions
cards.body: One: SPCX options on Tuesday bring hedging and real price discovery — debut-week IPOs often get volatile once puts exist. Two: a sector rally that its largest name (NVDA, flat at $205) refused to join twice is either late or telling you something. Gold's +3.3% close on a green equity day says positioning stays defensive underneath.
